FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class VirtualListEl

Namespace
FluentGpu.Reconciler
Assembly
FluentGpu.Reconciler.dll

A virtualized collection viewport: ONE retained node + a window of keyed children. Each frame the visible range is dirty, the reconciler asks the Layout for [first,last), calls RenderItem for just that window, keys each row, and feeds it to the existing keyed-LIS diff — so recycling IS CreateNode/FreeNode over the slab free-list (no second pool). Scroll is layout-free (the -ScrollOffset is the content's transform).

Layout is a pluggable IVirtualLayout (stack / grid / custom — pure, allocation-free). E11-L1: the viewport consumes BOTH seam kinds — an IMeasuredVirtualLayout gets the variable-extent estimate-then-correct arrange (measured rows + scroll anchoring) through the SAME property; when the layout is null, the legacy built-in Fenwick extent-table path runs (kept source/behavior-compatible). The user-facing Virtual.List/Grid/Custom/Measured/VariableList/… factories live in FluentGpu.Controls; this record is the engine primitive the reconciler diffs directly (ElementTypeId 6). See design/subsystems/virtualization.md.

public sealed record VirtualListEl : Element, IEquatable<Element>, IEquatable<VirtualListEl>

OnItemClearing

WinUI ItemsRepeater.ElementClearing — fired with the item index when it leaves the window.

public Action<int>? OnItemClearing { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<int>

OnItemIndexChanged

WinUI ItemsRepeater.ElementIndexChanged — a persistent bound slot rebound (oldIndex, newIndex).

public Action<int, int>? OnItemIndexChanged { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<int, int>

OnItemPrepared

WinUI ItemsRepeater.ElementPrepared — fired with the item index when it is realized.

public Action<int>? OnItemPrepared { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<int>

OnRealized

Called once when this viewport is realized into the scene, with its node handle — the escape hatch a composing control (ItemsView) uses to drive ScrollState (StartBringItemIntoView, sticky pinning).

public Action<NodeHandle>? OnRealized { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<NodeHandle>

OnVisibleRange

Prefetch hook: the realized window changed → (first, last) exclusive. Use to warm images/data just outside the viewport (the plan's UseVisibleRange surface). Fired only when the range actually moved.

public Action<int, int>? OnVisibleRange { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<int, int>

Overscan 

public int Overscan { get; init; }

Property Value

int

RenderItem 

public Func<int, Element> RenderItem { get; init; }

Property Value

Func<int, Element>

RowBind

Signals-first BOUND row template (the recycler fast path): the template runs ONCE per visible slot with an index SIGNAL; scrolling rebinds a slot by writing its signal, so only the row's reactive binds (TextBind/FillBind/SourceBind/…) re-run — zero element rebuild, zero reconcile, zero keys. When set, RenderItem/KeyOf are ignored.

public Func<IReadSignal<int>, Element>? RowBind { get; init; }

Property Value

Func<IReadSignal<int>, Element>

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
